## Summary

Adds `docs/architecture/pass3a-gate-validity-contract.md` to define terminal and gate-valid states for Pass 3A.

## Core rule

> Degraded is gate-valid only with structured proof. Failed is terminal but never gate-valid.

## What this specifies

- Allowed `pass3a_status` values
- Allowed `pass3a_gate_validity` values
- Recommended progress fields
- Done/degraded/failed semantics
- Derived Review Gate rule
- Phase 2 guard behavior
- Artifact invariants for `pass3_preflight_draft_v1`
- Logging rules for Pass 3A MAP/REDUCE/degraded/failed

## Why

Phase 2 must not outrun missing, running, half-written, or failed preflight. This contract prevents temporal correctness drift by requiring Pass 3A to be terminal-and-gate-valid before Review Gate or Phase 2.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `failed` is explicitly not gate-valid.
- [ ] `degraded` requires structured proof.
- [ ] `review_gate_ready` is specified as derived truth.
- [ ] Phase 2 guard semantics are documented.
